derived_from: Test vectors verified by running Python 3.14.3 sqlite3 module on macOS/Darwin 25.4.0, 2026-04-01
notes: All roundtrip tests use ':memory:' databases — no filesystem state is retained between tests.
complete_statement() returns True only when the SQL string ends with a semicolon and constitutes a syntactically complete statement.
Row values returned by fetchall() are tuples; the python_sqlite_roundtrip handler converts each row to a list for JSON comparison.
sqlite3.OperationalError is a subclass of sqlite3.DatabaseError, which is a subclass of Exception.

library.notes: sqlite3 is a Python stdlib module wrapping the bundled SQLite library. The bundled SQLite version may differ from the system-installed one. All behavioral invariants here use the in-process ':memory:' mode.

special_values.name: PARSE_DECLTYPES
special_values.description: Flag: parse declared column types for type detection

special_values.name: PARSE_COLNAMES
special_values.description: Flag: parse column names for type detection

connection.description: Database connection returned by sqlite3.connect(). Must be closed or used as a context manager when done.

cursor.description: Cursor object for executing SQL statements and iterating results.

sqlite3.connect.description: Open a connection to an SQLite database. ':memory:' creates a transient in-process database that is destroyed when the connection closes.

parameters.name: database
parameters.description: Path to database file, or ':memory:' for an in-process database.

sqlite3.complete_statement.description: Return True if the string sql contains one or more complete SQL statements terminated by semicolons. This does not parse or validate the SQL; it only checks for syntactic completeness.

parameters.name: sql
parameters.description: SQL string to test for completeness.

connection.execute.description: Shorthand for creating a cursor and calling its execute() method. Returns the cursor.

cursor.fetchall.description: Fetch all remaining rows of a query result, returning a list of tuples. Returns an empty list if no rows are available.

invariants.description: complete_statement('SELECT 1;') == True — semicolon-terminated statement is complete
complete_statement('SELECT 1') == False — statement missing semicolon is not complete
complete_statement('SELECT * FROM t;') == True — complete SELECT with semicolon
complete_statement('INSERT INTO t VALUES (1') == False — unclosed parenthesis is incomplete
complete_statement('') == False — empty string is not a complete statement
CREATE TABLE with INTEGER and TEXT columns, INSERT two rows, SELECT returns both rows in order

setup_sql: CREATE TABLE t (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, val TEXT)
spec.query_sql: SELECT id, val FROM t ORDER BY id

invariants.description: NULL is stored and retrieved as Python None — NULL semantics preserved through roundtrip

setup_sql: CREATE TABLE nulltest (id INTEGER, val TEXT)

invariants.description: REAL column stores and retrieves floating-point values accurately

setup_sql: CREATE TABLE prices (item TEXT, price REAL)
spec.query_sql: SELECT item, price FROM prices ORDER BY item

invariants.description: COUNT(*) aggregate over inserted rows returns correct integer count
WHERE clause correctly filters rows — only matching rows are returned

setup_sql: CREATE TABLE scores (name TEXT, score INTEGER)
spec.query_sql: SELECT name FROM scores WHERE score >= 85 ORDER BY name

invariants.description: SELECT that matches no rows returns empty list — not an error
ORDER BY DESC returns rows in descending order

spec.query_sql: SELECT n FROM nums ORDER BY n DESC

invariants.description: complete_statement(42) raises TypeError — argument must be str, not int

error_model.return_code_semantics: sqlite3 uses Python exceptions rather than integer return codes.
error_model.error_stickiness: Connection and cursor objects do not enter a permanent error state; individual operations raise exceptions but the connection remains usable.

error_codes.name: sqlite3.OperationalError
error_codes.meaning: Raised for SQL-level errors: invalid syntax, missing tables, constraint violations, etc.

error_codes.name: sqlite3.DatabaseError
error_codes.meaning: Base class for all database-related errors. sqlite3.OperationalError is a subclass.

error_codes.name: sqlite3.IntegrityError
error_codes.meaning: Raised when a database integrity constraint (PRIMARY KEY, UNIQUE, FOREIGN KEY, NOT NULL, CHECK) is violated.
